response.justification: Candidate brings strong technical leadership credentials and deep infrastructure experience with distributed systems and cloud technologies, but falls short on the explicit minimum requirement of 8 years of C++ programming (has only ~6 months at National Instruments, 2014-2015). Recent career trajectory has shifted toward TypeScript/web infrastructure rather than systems-level work. Infrastructure expertise is genuinely relevant and leadership capabilities are proven, but the C++ gap represents a significant risk for a role specifically requiring this depth.
strengths: Extensive distributed systems and infrastructure expertise (Dunnhumby: microservices at millions of RPS; low-latency systems with async I/O and caching)
Proven technical leadership across multiple Staff-level roles with demonstrated mentoring, organizing developer workshops, and driving engineering practices
Production cloud architecture experience (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deployment, real-world infrastructure at scale)
concerns: Critical gap on minimum requirement: only ~6 months of C++ programming versus 8 years required (last C++ work was ~10 years ago)
Recent technical focus has shifted from systems/infrastructure work to TypeScript, Node.js, and web-based platforms, moving away from compute-level systems
Only Bachelor's degrees (no Master's or PhD) against preferred qualification for this senior technical leadership role
